full_text:
A) Dissociation

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by Maria, Aino and Foteini.

The sample was divided in two parts and the dispase dissociation procedure was applied to both parts as follows:

Dispase dissociation media included 29,1 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 600 µl Dispase + 300 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

The Collagenase & Hyaluronidase dissociation procedure was applied to both parts as follows:

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 26,7 ml DMEM/F12+++, 3 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 300 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C, rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Dispase dissociated cells:

(1) First RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. Second RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet (part) was resuspended in 20 mL of icPDC media (1:5 dilution) for experiment. The final pellet (part) was resuspended in 20 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media (1:10 dilution) for Biobanking.

Sequentially dissociated cells: (1) First RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. Second RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ (1:5 dilution). The final pellet was resuspended in 20 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media.

Cell counts: For Experiment

Cell counts: For biobanking

B) Magnetic Activated Cell Sorting MACs

Negative Isolation

Performed negative isolation for the obtained sequentially dissociated cells.

Negative isolation: Depleting the sample from cancer and immune cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: (29,73 x 5 = 148,65 )ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and CaCl2) + 750 µl FBS + 600 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Based on the cell count, divide the cell suspension into equal fractions so that the maximum number of cells does not exceed the recommended sample size. Recommended sample size for leukocytes: 0.1 E6 - 100 E6 labeled cells in 10 E6 - 2000 E6 total cells per column. Four (4) equal fractions were calculated, requiring 2.208 µl of cold MACS buffer for a total of 240E6 cells, specifically 60E6 cells per column.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 2.208 µl of cold MACS buffer (calculation: the desired volume [500 µl] - the volume of both beads [2x 20 µl each type]). Added 480 µl of both anti-EpCAM and 480 µl anti-CD45 beads, respectively, to the cell suspension and incubated 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 24 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm (408 g) for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500µl x 4 (fractions) of MACS buffer.

Placed three LS columns and 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged 1500 rpm g for 5 min. Resuspended the flow through cells in 5mL icPDC media. Resuspended the column bound cells in 10 mL DMEM/F12+++ media. Cells in icPDC media (fibroblast-enriched fraction) were then subjected to dead cell removal (see next section), while the cells resuspended in DMEM/F12+++ (cancer and immune cell-enriched fraction) were used for biobanking (see biobanking section).

Cell count: Fibroblast

C) Dead Cell Removal

Dead cell depletion: cells are magnetically labeled with Dead Cell Removal MicroBeads and passed through a separation column. The unlabeled living cells run through.

Overview:

Prepare 1x Binding Buffer by dilution of 20x Binding Buffer Stock Solution with sterile, double-distiled water, 25 mL final volume of 1x Binding Buffer was prepared, 1.25 mL of Binding Buffer + 23.75mL of ddH2O.

Collect cells, centrifuge at 300 g and remove supernatant completely.

Resuspend cells with Dead cell Removal Microbeads, 541.4 μl beads. Mix well and incubate for 15 min at room temperature.

Place column in a MACs Separator. Prepare by rinsing with 1x Binding Buffer.

Apply cell suspensions onto the column. Rinse with 1x Binding Buffer.

Collect effluent as live cell fraction.

Cell count – Live Cells in 2mL icPDC media

Combining dispase-dissociated cells with enriched CAFs

Calculated the ratio of dispase-dissociated cells to flow-through cells (enriched CAFs), using the live cell counts. Ratio 1:1 – 9.6E6 cells x (mL/9.255E6 cells) = 1.04mL Fibroblasts (live).

Add the calculated volumes of both cell suspensions together. Calculated the concentration of the resulting combined cell suspension.

iPDC Experimental Conditions

Cell Density: 2.55 × 10⁶ cells/mL

A. Chromium

B. Flow Cytometry

icPDC Experimental Conditions

Cell Density: 3.99 × 10⁶ cells/mL

A. Chromium

B. Flow Cytometry

Biobanking

Dispase dissociated cells: 7.5E6 cells/vial total 6 vials

Sequentially dissociated cells and immune & cancer cells (after enrichment): 10E6 cells/vial total 6 vials

Cells were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

Experiment

Sample was processed for downstream Chromium single-cell analysis, Flow Cytometry, and sequential scRNA-seq to evaluate CAF-mediated drug responses.→See 15_Log_file_wet_lab on Google drive
